<!-- pinned: 2026-06-11 -->
### Gotcha: rtk pytest directory-glob false negative
rtk-proxied `pytest tests/` (directory glob) can report "No tests collected" with exit 0 — an rtk wrapper artifact, NOT a real collection failure. Before concluding a suite is broken: re-run with an explicit file path or bypass rtk. Confirmed twice in PR #1 re-review (eval suite actually 53 passed).

### 2026-06-11 11:00
**Context**: Session orchestration retrospective for feature #2 (Phase 1 experiment pipeline — paper 2: SFT vs DPO vs KTO abstention), team pact-6d29f2e2, captured by the secretary during the Consolidation Harvest / wrap-up (Pass 2, task #77, 2026-06-11). This is the whole-session arc: PREPARE -> ARCHITECT -> CODE -> TEST -> REVIEW (PR #1) -> remediation cycle 1 -> verify-only re-review -> MERGE (PR #1, 050bfd6). The feature spanned a research repo (Epistemic-Humility-Research) plus a git submodule (synaptic-tuner, kept experiment-agnostic). Consolidation safety-net swept the peer-review reviewer HANDOFFs (#56/#58/#60/#62) and verify-only re-review HANDOFFs (#70-73), which were NOT in a prior standard harvest.
**Goal**: Record the full-session orchestration arc, the safety-net confirmation that the late reviewer/re-review HANDOFFs were already captured by prior harvests, and the cross-phase calibration finding (3 of 5 ml-experiment-pipeline samples), so a future session resuming this multi-paper research program has the institutional through-line and the calibration cohort state.
**Decisions**: Consolidation Pass 2: safety-net sweep of reviewer HANDOFFs #56/#58/#60/#62 and verify-only re-reviews #70-73 produced ZERO net-new institutional memories — every reviewer finding was the ORIGINAL finding already remediated in cycle 1 (#74) and already distilled. Mapping confirmed against docs/review/pr1-phase1-pipeline-review.md: B1 -> 8cea5da (disposition-prose-ambiguity); FB1/FB2/MB4/MA1-target -> f2e0361 (silent-substitution); answer_value M1/F1 -> 950f15e; MS1/MS2/FS1 -> 7f0e1b7 + f2e0361 + 950f15e; severity-follows-crash-trace -> ae205adc; rtk pytest directory-glob artifact -> 950f15e + project pin. The re-reviews #70-73 were verify-only 4/4 ALL_RESOLVED confirmations carrying no new content., No memory merges or prunes warranted: each session memory is a distinct topic (silent-substitution bug class / method-registration / recipe-SSOT / cloud seed-beta saga / OpenMOSS rails / commit-gate reword / org-state / disposition-prose / remediation-contracts / render-execution-authority / crossed-message-latency / 3 calibration samples). f2e0361 grew large but is a cohesive one-class narrative (silent-substitution, 7 instances), left intact — same call made for f34137e7 in a prior session., Calibration cohort state for the ml-experiment-pipeline domain: 3 of 5 samples saved (CODE edeb85a7, TEST cf4869fc, REVIEW ae205adc). Learning II NOT yet activated (threshold 5). Held discrete + cross-linked, never merged.
**Lessons**: WHOLE-SESSION ARC: a single research-pipeline feature ran cleanly through all five PACT phases plus a full peer-review remediation loop and merged as PR #1 (050bfd6). The shape that worked: CODE distilled per-theme not per-HANDOFF (13 HANDOFFs -> 7 memories); TEST ran two remediation waves to GREEN; REVIEW used 4 parallel reviewers by concern, consolidated 7 fixers by file ownership (0 collisions, 0 fix-rejection cycles), and closed with cross-paired verify-only re-review (4/4 ALL_RESOLVED). Merge != verification — user manual test still pending at session close., CROSS-PHASE CALIBRATION THROUGH-LINE: CODE and TEST both UNDER-estimated uncertainty by +1 (different mechanisms: dependency-internals; external-artifact-decay + template-render-ground-truth), but REVIEW scored ACCURATE. The unifying hypothesis is that uncertainty drift is SURFACE-DEPENDENT, not a universal scoring bias: open-ended surfaces (a dependency's internal API, externally-pinned hub names, tokenizer render output) carry latent uncertainty the variety scorer misses, whereas diff-bounded review work does not. Future ml-experiment-pipeline dispatches should bump uncertainty +1 when the critical path runs through any of those open-ended surfaces., HARVEST-IDEMPOTENCY held all session: harvest assignments (#37/#53/#74/#75) were each RE-DELIVERED a turn after completion; verifying task status + memory presence before acting (rather than re-processing) avoided duplicate saves every time. This idempotent check-first discipline was never corrected => validated. See [[harvest-patterns]]., SAFETY-NET sweep is mostly a CONFIRMATION pass when an incremental harvest already covered the remediation cycle: because reviewer findings ARE the findings that get remediated, harvesting the remediation cycle (#74) captures the institutional content, and the later reviewer/re-review HANDOFF sweep confirms coverage rather than adding memories. Budget the consolidation pass as verification, not net-new distillation, when an incremental harvest preceded it., PROJECT-SCOPE GOTCHA for the secretary: pact-memory project_id is derived from cwd at save time. When operating from a git WORKTREE (.worktrees/phase1-pipeline -> project_id '.claude'), saves/searches land in the wrong scope; run the CLI from the MAIN project dir (Epistemic-Humility-Research) so memories associate with project_id 'Epistemic-Humility-Research' and stay findable by future sessions.
**Reasoning chains**: Consolidation Pass 2 must safety-net the un-harvested reviewer HANDOFFs #56/#58/#60/#62 + re-reviews #70-73 -> but those reviewer findings are the SAME findings already remediated in cycle 1 and harvested in #74 -> so the sweep maps each to an existing memory (verified against the synthesis doc) and finds ZERO net-new -> therefore the consolidation deliverable is one retrospective memory + tracking update, not a batch of new finding-memories., CODE under-estimated uncertainty +1, TEST under-estimated +1, REVIEW accurate -> the difference is the SURFACE: CODE/TEST uncertainty came from open-ended surfaces (dependency internals, externally-pinned artifacts, render ground-truth) while REVIEW was bounded by an already-written diff -> so the calibration rule is surface-conditioned (+1 when the critical path is an open-ended surface), and the 3-sample cohort is internally consistent on that reading.
